title: geranium pratense subsp. sylvaticum
intro: Geranium pratense subsp. sylvaticum, also known as Wood Crane's-bill or Wild Geranium, is a subspecies of the Geranium pratense plant, which is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Wood Crane's-bill include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for women's health issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
